## reference · ux legibility readability comprehension measurable
* **Legibility**: ensure text is clear and distinguishable.
	+ Use a reasonably large default font size (avoid tiny text).
	+ Allow users to change font size.
	+ High contrast between characters and background.
	+ Plain background (avoid busy or textured).
	+ Clean typeface (avoid strangely shaped fonts).
* **Readability**: measure complexity of words and sentence structure.
	+ Use plainspoken words (shorter is better).
	+ Short sentences (avoid convoluted structures).
	+ Active voice (except in rare circumstances).
	+ Target 8th-grade reading level for broad consumer audience.
	+ Target 12th-grade reading level for educated or specialized B2B audience.
* **Comprehension**: measure user understanding of text.
	+ Use user-centric language.
	+ Specialized terminology for specialized audience.
	+ Inverted-pyramid writing style (start with conclusion).
	+ Minimize cognitive load.
	+ Use pictures or diagrams to explain complex concepts.
	+ Be brief (especially for mobile users).
* Testing:
	+ Legibility: measure reading speed (words per minute).
	+ Readability: use computer-based readability calculators (e.g., Flesch-Kincaid).
	+ Comprehension: standard user testing, realistic tasks, and exams.

Sources: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/legibility-readability-comprehension/
